data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify employs a microservices architecture allowing independent deployment and scaling of services.
data.technical_spec: Each service runs its own process and communicates with lightweight mechanisms, often an HTTP resource API.

data.title: Event-Driven Systems
data.description: Spotify uses event-driven systems to handle asynchronous communications and updates across different services.
data.technical_spec: Kafka and Pub/Sub are integral for streaming logs and events.

data.title: Client-Server Model
data.description: A robust client-server model that enables seamless music streaming and user interactions.
data.technical_spec: RESTful APIs serve as the backbone for data interaction between client and backend services.

data.title: Cloud Native Architecture
data.description: Spotify is mostly hosted on cloud platforms enabling flexible scaling and high availability.
data.technical_spec: Utilization of container orchestration platforms like Kubernetes for deployment.

data.title: Data-Partitioning
data.description: Ensures efficient management and retrieval of user and song data across distributed databases.
data.technical_spec: Sharding techniques are used to partition data across global servers.
